name: User Account Creation followed by Account Deletion (Windows)
notes: This rule triggers when a user is created and deleted within a short period of time. This could indicate an attacker or malware trying to hide or evade detection by using different user accounts or to drop a bomb on the system.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Deleted (Windows) match at least 1 times in 15 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Added (Windows) match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Deleted (Windows) match at least 1 times in 15 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Added (Windows) match with the same Machine Identifier (custom), Target Username (custom)
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Deleted (Windows) match at least 1 times in 15 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Added (Windows) match with the same Machine Identifier (custom), Target Username (custom)
TD_list: APPLY User Account Creation followed by Account Deletion (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Deleted (Windows) match at least 1 times in 15 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: User Account Added (Windows) match with the same Machine Identifier (custom), Target Username (custom)
RED_list: Event name: User Account Creation followed by Account Deletion
Event description: Detects when a user is created and deleted within a short period of time.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
